Run the first Brenner Loop session on `bio_inspired_nanochat`:
- kickoff → agent responses (structured deltas) → compile → publish artifact

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.10.2.3","title":"Task: Run an experiment-backed round using capture→encode→compile","description":"# Goal
Validate “Science Mode” end-to-end on a real target by running at least one discriminative test and feeding the result back into the session artifact.

This is the proof that the lab is not just producing artifacts, but can actually *update on reality*.

---

# Success criteria (minimum)
- One thread with:
  - role-separated kickoff (or explicitly documented roster)
  - at least one discriminative test T# in the artifact
  - a real command executed by the operator
  - captured `ExperimentResult` JSON file
  - a DELTA message produced by `experiment encode` (or `experiment post`)
  - a compiled artifact updated to reflect the run metadata

---

# Notes / guardrails
- Keep the experiment harmless and reproducible.
- Prefer a test that can be run on a laptop in <5 minutes.
- If results are ambiguous, quarantine them as such; don’t force a narrative.

---

# Output
- Link the thread id
- List the exact commands run
- Commit/persist the updated artifact in the target workspace (if appropriate)
","notes":"Completed pilot thread RS-20251231-bionanochat-rrp-vs-freq (project_key=/data/projects/bio_inspired_nanochat).

Key commands:
- ./brenner.ts session start --project-key /data/projects/bio_inspired_nanochat --sender BrownSnow --to BrownSnow,GreenDog --thread-id RS-20251231-bionanochat-rrp-vs-freq --role-map 'BrownSnow=hypothesis_generator,GreenDog=test_designer' --excerpt-file /data/projects/bio_inspired_nanochat/artifacts/RS-20251231-bionanochat-rrp-vs-freq/excerpt.md --question '...' --context '...'
- ./brenner.ts mail send ... delta_add_test_T1.md (adds T1)
- ./brenner.ts experiment run --thread-id RS-20251231-bionanochat-rrp-vs-freq --test-id T1 --cwd /data/projects/bio_inspired_nanochat -- uv run python artifacts/RS-20251231-bionanochat-rrp-vs-freq/presyn_edge_dependence.py
- ./brenner.ts experiment post --project-key /data/projects/bio_inspired_nanochat --result-file artifacts/RS-20251231-bionanochat-rrp-vs-freq/experiments/T1/20251231T092732Z_f00fc4f9-ff6a-406b-9941-36952bd39607.json --sender BrownSnow --to BrownSnow,GreenDog
- ./brenner.ts mail send ... delta_fill_minimum_artifact.md (fills RT/H/P/A/C, adds T2, adds T1 score)
- ./brenner.ts session write --project-key /data/projects/bio_inspired_nanochat --thread-id RS-20251231-bionanochat-rrp-vs-freq --out-file /data/projects/bio_inspired_nanochat/artifacts/RS-20251231-bionanochat-rrp-vs-freq/artifact.md

Outputs:
- ExperimentResult JSON: /data/projects/bio_inspired_nanochat/artifacts/RS-20251231-bionanochat-rrp-vs-freq/experiments/T1/20251231T092732Z_f00fc4f9-ff6a-406b-9941-36952bd39607.json
- Compiled artifact: /data/projects/bio_inspired_nanochat/artifacts/RS-20251231-bionanochat-rrp-vs-freq/artifact.md (v1, linter VALID)
- Micro-sim metric: mean_abs_diff_uniform_vs_random=0.1884, random_repeat=0.0 (suggests edge dependence in presynaptic forward() under this synthetic setup).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownSnow","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:44:36.165416593-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T04:34:33.036970098-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T04:34:33.036970098-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.10.2.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.10.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:44:36.194710223-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.10.2.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:00.099901123-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.10.2.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:05.171523661-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.10.2.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1es5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:10.242472531-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.10.2.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:15.314562077-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.11","title":"EPIC: Bootstrap + distribution (installers, CI, releases)","description":"# Problem
Right now, getting a fresh machine to a *working BrennerBot operator setup* is high-friction and error-prone:

- We rely on several **external but required** cockpit tools (at minimum: `ntm`, `cass`, `cm`) plus our own CLI (`brenner`).
- Each tool has its own install and verification steps.
- Without a single “happy path”, new users (and future-us) will drift into snowflake setups.

# Goal
Ship a **single, safe, reproducible bootstrap story** that:

1. Provides a **curl|bash one-liner** to install the BrennerBot operator toolchain.
2. Ensures `ntm`, `cass`, and `cm` are installed and *working* (verification step).
3. Installs the `brenner` CLI as a **standalone binary** (via `bun build --compile` release artifacts).
4. Adds **CI + release pipelines** so installs are continuously tested and releases are deterministic.

# Why this matters
BrennerBot is “documents-first”, but it’s also an **operator-driven multi-agent lab**.
If the cockpit toolchain is painful to set up, the project won’t be used.

# Design principles
- **No vendor AI APIs** in code. Bootstrap installs tools; it does not wire vendor inference.
- **Fail-soft**: installation should offer `--skip-*` toggles, but default should be the full operator toolchain.
- **Security**: installers must verify checksums for downloaded binaries.
- **Reproducibility**: support pinned versions via a toolchain manifest.
- **CI parity**: installers must be testable non-interactively and in CI (ideally using file:// artifacts).

# Scope (this epic)
- `install.sh` and (optional) `install.ps1` for BrennerBot.
- GitHub Actions:
  - CI (lint/build/tests + installer smoke tests)
  - Release (tagged builds + checksums + GitHub release assets)
- A “doctor” / preflight check command to verify the toolchain.
- A toolchain manifest that pins known-good versions of `ntm`, `cass`, `cm`, and `brenner`.

# Non-goals
- Installing proprietary CLI agents (Codex/Claude/Gemini) — those are subscription tools.
- Auto-starting Agent Mail server (but we can check its reachability).
- Bulk data generation (corpus indexes, etc.) — separate epics.

# Reference implementations (models)
We will explicitly emulate patterns from:
- `Dicklesworthstone/ntm` (curl installer + CI + release)
- `Dicklesworthstone/coding_agent_session_search` (cass) (installer verification + dist pipeline)
- `Dicklesworthstone/cass_memory_system` (cm) (Bun cross-compile release + installer checksum)
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:35:46.517709942-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T14:08:54.488705818-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T14:08:54.488705818-05:00","close_reason":"EPIC complete: toolchain manifest, installer, doctor/upgrade, CI, docs, and repo hygiene all implemented","labels":["ci","installer","ops","release","toolchain"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:35:46.528027021-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.1","title":"Toolchain manifest: pin ntm/cass/cm (+ bun) versions","description":"# Goal
Create a **single source of truth** for the BrennerBot operator toolchain versions.

We need to pin versions so that:
- `curl | bash` installs are reproducible
- CI can validate “known-good” combinations
- future-us can update tool versions intentionally (not accidentally)

# Background
We depend on external tools:
- `ntm` (cockpit orchestration)
- `cass` (session index/search)
- `cm` (procedural memory)

Each ships its own installer and release artifacts. Those installers support `--version` flags, but *without a manifest* we will constantly drift.

# Proposal
Add a repo file (format TBD; JSON or TOML) that declares:

- Tool name
- Repo URL
- Pinned version tag (semver)
- Supported platforms (Linux/macOS/Windows) and any exclusions (e.g., `ntm` is effectively Unix-only)
- Install strategy:
  - preferred: download release asset + verify checksum
  - fallback: call upstream install script at a pinned tag
- Verification command(s)

This manifest becomes the default input for `install.sh --toolchain <manifest>` and for `brenner doctor`.

# Key design choices
- **Pin by tag**, not by `main` branch.
- **Checksum verification** is required for binary downloads.
- Prefer “operator-safe defaults”: install into `~/.local/bin` unless `--system`.

# Acceptance criteria
- Toolchain manifest exists and is documented.
- A human can read it and understand exactly what will be installed.
- `install.sh` can consume it (planned in sibling beads).
- CI has at least one job that validates the manifest parses and that “install plan rendering” works.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:36:06.520113533-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T13:15:49.372065318-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T13:15:49.372065318-05:00","close_reason":"All P1 children complete","labels":["cass","cm","installer","ntm","security","toolchain"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:36:06.548656987-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.1.1","title":"Define toolchain manifest schema + initial pinned versions","description":"# Output
Produce the initial toolchain manifest with **explicit pinned versions** for:
- `brenner` (this repo)
- `ntm`
- `cass`
- `cm`
- minimum required `bun` version (if relevant for dev builds)

# Considerations
- `ntm` is Unix-only (tmux); include platform metadata.
- Prefer pinned tags known to work together.
- Decide where this file lives (repo root vs `scripts/` vs `specs/`).

# Acceptance criteria
- Manifest file exists in repo.
- It contains: repo URL, tag/version, and platform notes for each tool.
- It is referenced by the installer bead (5so.11.2).
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:37:41.039394255-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:49:45.293465975-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:49:45.293465975-05:00","close_reason":"Manifest file exists at specs/toolchain.manifest.json with pinned versions for brenner, ntm, cass, cm, and bun. Format spec is in specs/toolchain_manifest_v0.1.md.","labels":["security","toolchain"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.1.1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:37:41.070464414-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.1.1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:40:00.945646768-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
